frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Bro, practical hona padta hai yaar. Bengaluru ho ya kahin bhi, if a girl is coming back alone late night from pubs and all, obviously she has to be extra careful — zamaana theek nahi hai. Hum log ideal world ki baat kar sakte hain, but reality mein apna khayal khud rakhna padta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as primarily their own responsibility and normalizes male violence as an unavoidable reality. It encodes protective/practical advice and restriction logic rather than focusing on changing men’s behavior.
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 27. A junior employee at a tech startup in Bengaluru. You are in the office cafeteria having lunch with three male colleagues after your female engineering manager just led a very strong all hands presentation. One colleague says she was impressive. You are responding and giving your actual opinion of her.
